name: Peña Cabrera Route
name_original: Ruta de Peña Cabrera — circular desde Santolaya
description: Hike the Peña Cabrera Route, a moderate 5.8-mile trail in Asturias with panoramic views of the Villaviciosa estuary and rural charm.
region_intro: Hiking around Cabranes, Asturias, offers diverse natural landscapes characterized by dramatic river gorges, lush Atlantic forests, and significant river systems. The region features elevated points providing panoramic views of the Villaviciosa estuary and the surrounding green Asturian countryside. Trails often wind through magical forests and along rivers like the Marea and Profundu, showcasing a blend of natural beauty and historical elements. Best hiking trails around Cabranes The most popular hiking route is Pendón River Gorges Trail, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 45 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads through dramatic river gorges and lush Atlantic forests. Another top favourite among local hikers is Route of the Mills of the Río Profundu — circular by the GR-108, a moderate 9.2 miles (14.8 km) path. This trail explores significant river systems and historical elements within the natural setting. Local hikers also love the La Peridiella Trail, a 6.6 miles (10.7 km) trail leading through magical forests and along the Marea River, often completed in about 2 hours 59 minutes. Hiking around Cabranes is defined by dramatic river gorges, lush Atlantic forests, and panoramic viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Cabranes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 120 reviews. More than 900 hikers have used komoot to explore Cabranes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Cabranes?
A: Cabranes offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 60 routes available. These range from easy strolls to more challenging ascents, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Cabranes?
A: Hiking in Cabranes will immerse you in diverse natural landscapes. You'll encounter dramatic river gorges, lush Atlantic forests, and significant river systems. Many trails also lead to elevated points offering panoramic views of the Villaviciosa estuary and the verdant Asturian countryside.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Cabranes?
A: Yes, Cabranes has several easy routes suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353005163/grases-and-the-valdedios-river-circular-from-amandi" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grases and the Valdediós river — circular from Amandi&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.9 km trail with minimal elevation gain, perfect for a leisurely family walk along the river.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Cabranes?
A: Many trails in Cabranes are suitable for dogs, but it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or protected wildlife. The natural environment, with its forests and rivers, provides a great experience for pets.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Cabranes?
A: Yes, Cabranes features several circular routes. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1352520496/route-of-the-mills-of-the-rio-profundu-circular-by-the-gr-108" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Mills of the Río Profundu — circular by the GR-108&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 14.8 km path that explores historical elements and river systems.

Q: Where can I find scenic viewpoints along the hiking trails?
A: For breathtaking panoramic views, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1352649271/pena-cabrera-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Cabrera Route&lt;/a&gt;. This trail offers incredible vistas of the entire council of Cabranes and the Villaviciosa estuary, showcasing the typical green Asturian landscape.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see while hiking in Cabranes?
A: Yes, the region around Cabranes is home to several beautiful waterfalls. You can explore highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4694980" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1821428" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molín de la Peña and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6039701" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llames Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5029162" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villamayor Recreation Area and El Chorrón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is also a popular spot.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can discover cultural and historical landmarks. These include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1127876" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Salvador de Priesca&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1132118" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa María de Valdediós&lt;/a&gt;. The traditional Asturian architecture, like the *hórreo* in Viñón, also adds to the charm of the area.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Cabranes?
A: Cabranes is beautiful year-round, but spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant conditions for hiking, with mild temperatures and vibrant natural colors. The lush forests and river systems are especially appealing during these seasons.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Cabranes?
A: The routes in Cabranes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 120 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's dramatic river gorges, lush forests, and the panoramic views from elevated points.

Q: Are there any trails that follow rivers or feature mills?
A: Yes, Cabranes is known for its river routes and historical mills. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058130181/la-peridiella-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Peridiella Trail&lt;/a&gt; winds along the Marea River through magical forests, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1352520496/route-of-the-mills-of-the-rio-profundu-circular-by-the-gr-108" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Mills of the Río Profundu&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights the area's historical milling heritage.

Q: What is the difficulty level of hikes in Cabranes?
A: Cabranes offers a range of difficulty levels. You'll find easy walks, moderate trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1048583178/pendon-river-gorges-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pendón River Gorges Trail&lt;/a&gt;, and some more challenging ascents. There are 22 easy, 30 moderate, and 8 difficult routes to choose from.
tips: While you climb the Peña Cabrera, the incredible views you have towards the south of the entire council of Cabranes are worth highlighting. And this is a very typical landscape of Asturias, so green and reflecting nature in its purest form.

This horreo that you can find in the small Asturian town of Viñón is one of the typical buildings in the area.
In addition, it is located right next to the Church of San Julián, which is a temple that can be included within the rural Romanesque, of approximately 12th century origin.

faqs.question: Where does the Peña Cabrera Route start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The route begins in Santolaya de Cabranes, Asturias, right next to the Santa Eulalia church. Parking is generally available in the village, but it's advisable to arrive early during peak seasons.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail progressively ascends through rural paths, passing between farms, meadows, and mixed forests of oaks, chestnuts, and eucalyptus. Higher sections around Valbuena are more open, offering clear views. Expect a mix of natural paths and some rural tracks.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy views of the Viacaba and Profundo river valleys. From the summit and the Peña Cabrera recreational area, there are extensive panoramic views of the Villaviciosa estuary and the central-eastern Asturian region, including the View of Cabranes. The route also passes by the historic Hórreo of Viñón.

faqs.question: Is the Peña Cabrera Route suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is generally dog-friendly. Given its rural paths and natural environment, it's a great option for hiking with your canine companion. Always keep your dog under control, especially when passing through farms or near livestock.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The route offers a complete experience of traditional Asturian nature and landscape, making it enjoyable across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant for their mild temperatures and vibrant colors. Summer can be warm, so an early start is recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Peña Cabrera Route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Is this a circular route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Peña Cabrera Route is a circular trail, allowing you to return to your starting point in Santolaya de Cabranes without retracing your steps. This circular option also allows you to pass through the village of Viñón.

faqs.question: What are some notable features or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Beyond the natural beauty, the route offers cultural insights. The starting point in Santolaya features the Santa Eulalia church, an excellent example of Asturian religious architecture. If you take the circular option through Viñón, you can visit the Museum of Rural School of Asturias and the Romanesque church of San Julián, which is a Provincial Monument of Historic-Artistic Interest.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the hike?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 9.4 kilometers long and typically takes around 2 hours and 50 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops for views.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hike?
faqs.answer: This route is considered to have a moderate difficulty. It involves a cumulative elevation gain of about 330 meters, with varied terrain that includes ascents and descents through rural paths and forests.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or restaurants nearby?
faqs.answer: Santolaya de Cabranes, the starting point, is a small village. For more options, the nearby village of Viñón, which is part of the circular route, might offer some local establishments. It's always a good idea to check opening hours in advance, especially outside of peak tourist season.
